rulings.Case Title: PHOENIX LAKE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. GLOBE PROTECT, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Enter Judgment (Code Civ. Proc. Sec 664.6)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 19 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF PHOENIX LAKE, INC., Motion To Enter Judgment (Code Civ. Proc. Sec 664.6). The motion is granted. The evidence indicates that defendant has breached Section 6(b) of the parties' settlement by failing to provide an accurate capitalization table to plaintiff. The declaration of Angelica Relucio shows that she has an ownership interest in defendant and that information was never disclosed to the plaintiff. rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE M. CRITSER et al VS. HEWLETT-PACKARD COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Special Interrogatories, Set One And Req. For Production Of Documents, Set One And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 19, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MICHELLE CRITSER's Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Special Interrogatories, Set One And Req. For Production Of Documents, Set One And Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. (A) Motion to Compel Special Interrogatories granted in part and denied in part. The information sought is relevant given the allegations of the Complaint and possible electrical device origin of the fire. Plaintiff's responses to discovery relied on by HP were the specific facts known to her as of that time, without prejudice to further discovery, and are not admissions that all other electrical device sources of the fire have been eliminated, as asserted by HP. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to Motion to Compel Further Responses to Special Interrogatories: Granted in part only as to 23, 24, 27, 31, 35: only as to the identities of those persons responsible for quality control of each component part of the model of Daniel's computer and the model of Michelle's computer. Granted as to Special Interrogatories 19, 20; 39-52; 53-75 (to be responded to based upon the corrected SI references as noted by HP in its initial response); 76-98; 100-109 (re 108, only as to writings referencing any such acquisition of assets, not "relating" to); 111-119 (re 119, only as to writings referencing any such acquisition of assets, not "relating" to); 120-130 (re 130, only as to writings referencing any such acquisition of assets, not "relating" to); 131, 132-133 (based upon reference to SI 131), 134-141; 142 (only as to writings referencing those production specifications, not "relating" to them); 146 (only as to writings referencing any such acquisition of assets, not "relating" to); 147 (assuming the reference is to SI 146), 148 (only as to writings referencing those production specifications, not "relating" to them), 149 (based upon reference to SI 148), 151, 153; 164-165; 168-169; 171, 172-194; 195-212; 218-240; 241-263; 264-286; 287; 290 (as to all component parts, not just the batteries); 291; 292-296; 297, 300, 301; 302-308; 313-318; 322-324; 335, 342-366; 373-378; 384-388; 404-421, 426, 428-430 (to the extent HP is able to do so); 433-435 (to the extent HP is able to do so); 437-450; 453, 456, 458; 469-473, 475 (only as to writings referencing those design changes, not "relating" to them), 476; 482-486; 493-496, 497 (only as to writings referencing those recommended or intended uses, not "relating" to them) 498; 505-510; 511-518 (to the extent HP had knowledge of any such contention); 519-526 (to the extent HP had knowledge of any such contention); =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE M. CRITSER et al VS. HEWLETT-PACKARD COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Sanctions ( part 2 of 2 for tentative rulings only, duplicate entry) ); 527-529 (limited to claims of which HP was aware regarding its laptop computers, within the 5 years prior to the date of the response), 530 (only as to writings referencing any such claims, not "relating" to them), 531, 532, 533 (only as to writings referencing any such claims, not "relating" to them), 534; 552-568; 569; 581 (for the 5 years prior to the date of the response), 582-588; 589-600; 606-634; 635-637 (to the extent HP has knowledge of any such contention re recall); 638-643; 644-646 (to the extent HP has knowledge of any such contention re recall); 647-652 (as to laptop computers only); 653-654 (to the extent HP has knowledge of any such contention re recall). Denied in part as to SI 23, 24, 27, 31, 35 as overbroad in that the SI essentially asks for the identities of any person who ever had anything to do with any aspect at all of the model of Daniel's computer - but granted only as to the identities of those persons responsible for quality control of each component part of the model of Daniel's computer; Denied as to SI 28, 32, 36, 162, 163, 166, 167, 474, as overbroad. Denied as to 99, 110 as incomprehensible. Denied as to 392 for failure to set forth the referenced SI 389 in the Separate Statement; as to 570-574 for failure to set forth the subject SI's in the Separate Statement Denied as to 422-425; 427, 431-432, 436, 452, 457, 459, 460, and 656-665 as unduly burdensome. Denied as to 569-574, and 575-580 as unduly burdensome for vagueness. HP to provide a privilege log as to any documents claimed to be privileged. Said further responses and privilege log to be provided within 21 days of the date of this order. Defendants' objections to plaintiff's evidence are overruled. Plaintiff's objections to defendants' evidence and separate statement are overruled. Sanctions awarded in the sum of $5,500. HP failed to meet and confer in good faith to assist the parties and the Court in limiting the issues, failed to meet and confer as to the claim of overbroad definitions and other issues, and was not substantially justified in contending that plaintiff's previous discovery answers served to limit the issues framed by the pleadings; resulting in an inordinate amount of time spent by plaintiff and the Court to address issues that likely could have been resolved without being the subject of this motion. No tentative ruling available as to RPD's at this time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: PRAVIN PATEL et al VS. SHARAD SHANTILAL PATEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Fees And Costs Pursuant To Order Denying Motion To Strike First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 19 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF PRAVIN PATEL, GOVINDJI PATEL Motion For Fees And Costs Pursuant To Order Denying Motion To Strike First Amended Complaint. Motion granted. The Plaintiffs are awarded attorneys' fees and costs in their favor in the amount of $20,264.00. Contrary to Defendants' assertions, the Motion is not untimely. Plaintiffs raised the attorney fee issue while litigating the anti-SLAPP motion, not pursuant to this Motion, which seeks the determination of the amount of an award previously ordered by this Court. Thus, the time limits of Cal. Rule of Court 3.1702(b) are inapplicable. Further, the Court's Order denying the anti-SLAPP and awarding attorneys' fees and costs was sufficiently supported by findings of fact and citation to law. Finally, the amount requested by Plaintiffs was not reasonable to the extent that Plaintiffs sought fees for the preparation of a reply on this motion that was never filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.
